subject.name: hibrid
subject.category: local-first LLM router that sits under AI coding tools
differentiators: Local-first: cheap/loop calls run on local Ollama small model at zero marginal cost
No API key for the strong tier: reaches frontier models through the user's already-authenticated agent CLI (claude -p, codex, opencode, copilot) or session, not a metered per-token API key
Measures the machine at startup (RAM/VRAM/tok-s) and routes on measured latency, not the spec sheet
Routes by task axis (code vs general vs reasoning), picking the best local model for the detected task
Privacy: PII can be forced to stay local

tools.name: RouteLLM
tools.what_it_does: Open-source framework for serving and evaluating LLM routers; decides per-query between a strong and a weak model to cut cost while preserving quality.
tools.routing_mechanism: Trained routers on preference data: matrix factorization, BERT classifier, similarity-weighted ranking, causal LLM classifier. Cascade/binary strong-vs-weak routing.
tools.local_first: No (designed around API model pairs, e.g. GPT-4 Turbo vs Mixtral; can be pointed at local backends but not its design point)

savings_claims.claim: Up to ~85% cost reduction on MT Bench while maintaining 95% of GPT-4 quality; only ~14% of queries sent to the strong model
savings_claims.methodology: MT Bench, GPT-4 Turbo (strong) vs Mixtral 8x7B (weak), matrix-factorization router

savings_claims.claim: ~45% cost reduction on MMLU and ~35% on GSM8K at comparable quality
savings_claims.methodology: Same model pairing, BERT-based classifier router

tools.name: LiteLLM
tools.what_it_does: Python SDK + self-hosted proxy/AI gateway calling 100+ LLM APIs in OpenAI format, with cost tracking, virtual keys, guardrails, load balancing, fallbacks, logging.
tools.routing_mechanism: Operational routing: retries, fallbacks, load balancing across deployments. Not a quality/task classifier; routing is rule/availability/cost based.
tools.local_first: Partial (supports local Ollama and vLLM as providers, but it is a gateway, not a local-first decision engine)
tools.needs_api_keys: Yes for cloud providers; manages virtual keys; can call keyless local models
tools.open_source: Yes (MIT for core; some enterprise features gated)

savings_claims.claim: No headline quality-routing savings percentage published; value is cost visibility, budgets, and provider failover rather than a savings benchmark

tools.name: OpenRouter
tools.what_it_does: Hosted unified gateway to 300-400+ models via one API key and OpenAI-compatible endpoint; load-balances across providers, can pick cheapest provider, offers an Auto Router and a free-model router.
tools.routing_mechanism: Provider load balancing (price/uptime); :floor for cheapest provider; Auto Router (powered by NotDiamond) picks best model per prompt by complexity/task/capability.
tools.local_first: No (cloud service; passes prompts through OpenRouter)
tools.needs_api_keys: Yes (single OpenRouter key; pay-per-token, no markup claimed)
tools.open_source: No (proprietary service; some free models offered)

savings_claims.claim: No first-party quality-routing savings percentage; savings come from picking cheapest provider per model and free-model availability

tools.name: Martian
tools.what_it_does: Commercial LLM router/gateway that for any prompt finds the model giving the best result at lowest cost; markets itself as the first LLM router. Accenture-invested.
tools.routing_mechanism: Interpretability-based prediction of how models will perform without running them (uses compression/quantization/distillation/specialized models); user sets quality vs willingness-to-pay criteria. Also provider failover.
tools.open_source: Mostly no (commercial; a 'Martian Protocol' router repo exists separately)

savings_claims.claim: Markets significant cost reduction vs single all-purpose model; no specific first-party benchmark percentage located
savings_claims.methodology: uncertain / not published in sources found

tools.name: Not Diamond
tools.what_it_does: Predictive model-recommendation layer: trains a custom router that determines which of 60+ LLMs gives the highest-quality response to a query. Explicitly a recommender, not a gateway/proxy.
tools.routing_mechanism: Preference/ranking model trained on a large LLM evaluation dataset; meta-model learns when to call each LLM. Tradeoff knobs for cost/latency (cost_quality_tradeoff 0-10). Routing decision returned, call executed client-side.
tools.local_first: No (cloud recommender; but does NOT proxy your prompts/keys, and can route to your own custom/fine-tuned endpoints)
tools.needs_api_keys: API key for Not Diamond's recommendation service; your model keys stay client-side
tools.open_source: Partial (open-sourced a lightweight router preview Dec 2023; main product is hosted)

savings_claims.claim: Markets quality maximization plus cost/latency tradeoffs; no specific first-party savings percentage located in sources
savings_claims.methodology: uncertain / not published in sources found

tools.name: Unify.ai
tools.what_it_does: LLM dev platform that dynamically routes prompts to the best model balancing cost/latency/quality, with quality/cost/latency sliders and published benchmarks.
tools.routing_mechanism: Trained router benchmarked across datasets (Open Hermes, GSM8K, HellaSwag, MMLU, MT-Bench); user-controlled quality/cost/latency sliders.
tools.open_source: No (hosted product; open-source alternatives like LLMRouter exist)

savings_claims.claim: Claims router can beat individual endpoints on average across MT-Bench/MMLU/GSM8K/HellaSwag and cut cost by using small models for simple tasks; no single headline % located

tools.name: semantic-router
tools.what_it_does: Open-source superfast decision layer that routes requests by semantic meaning (which path/tool/LLM) using vector embeddings instead of an LLM call.
tools.routing_mechanism: Embeddings + kNN/cosine similarity against example utterances; encoders for OpenAI, Cohere, HuggingFace, FastEmbed; in-memory or Pinecone/Qdrant.
tools.local_first: Partial (can run local embedding encoders like FastEmbed/HuggingFace; routing math is local)
tools.needs_api_keys: Optional (only if using a hosted encoder; local encoders are keyless)

savings_claims.claim: No cost-savings % claim; value is latency (routing decision ~5000ms LLM -> ~100ms vector math) and avoiding LLM calls for routing

tools.name: Portkey AI Gateway
tools.what_it_does: Open-source, enterprise AI gateway routing to 1600+ models with one API; load balancing, retries/fallbacks, timeouts, guardrails, PII redaction, virtual keys, observability.
tools.routing_mechanism: Config-driven routing rules, weighted load balancing across keys/providers, fallbacks; can route to privately hosted/local models via custom host URLs. Operational, not quality/task classifier.
tools.local_first: Partial (can target local models via custom host; primarily a managed/self-hostable gateway)
tools.needs_api_keys: Yes for cloud providers; own keys or virtual keys; self-hosted has no per-request fee
tools.open_source: Yes (Apache-2.0 gateway, npx @portkey-ai/gateway / Docker); managed plan separate

savings_claims.claim: No quality-routing savings %; PII redaction guardrail and self-hosting (no per-request fees) are the cost/privacy levers

tools.name: GPTCache
tools.what_it_does: Open-source semantic cache for LLM apps: stores prior responses and serves cached answers for semantically similar queries, cutting calls, latency and cost.
tools.routing_mechanism: Not a model router; embedding-based semantic cache lookup (vector store + similarity). Complementary to routing.
tools.local_first: Partial (cache and local embedding models run locally; still calls the LLM on cache miss)
tools.needs_api_keys: Only for the underlying LLM on cache miss / hosted embeddings

savings_claims.claim: Semantic caching cuts inference cost ~30-70% by reuse; 2-10x faster on cache hit; one study reduced API calls up to 68.8% (hit rate 61.6-68.8%)
savings_claims.methodology: GPTCache paper + GPT Semantic Cache study (arxiv 2411.05276) across query categories

tools.name: LiteLLM + Ollama (local-first DIY pattern)
tools.what_it_does: The most-cited open-source local-first routing recipe: Ollama runs local models, LiteLLM (or n8n templates) routes routine tasks local and overflow/frontier tasks to cloud (e.g. OpenRouter).
tools.routing_mechanism: Manual/rule-based tiered routing (small/medium/large), optionally graduating to semantic routing. No hardware benchmarking or task-axis model selection out of the box.
tools.local_first: Yes (this is the local-first category hibrid competes in)
tools.needs_api_keys: Yes for the cloud tier (metered per-token API key); local tier keyless

savings_claims.claim: No formal benchmark; savings depend on share of traffic kept local. Note: since Ollama v0.14 (Jan 2026) Claude Code can connect directly to local models via Anthropic Messages API compatibility (no proxy)

positioning: hibrid's genuinely differentiated combination is: (1) reaching the strong tier through the user's already-authenticated agent CLI/session so there is no metered per-token API key for frontier calls, and (2) benchmarking the actual machine at startup and routing on measured local latency. No competitor researched does either. Its other claims (local-first, task-axis routing, OSS) each exist somewhere in the field but not bundled: the DIY LiteLLM+Ollama pattern is local-first but manual and still needs a cloud API key; RouteLLM/Unify/NotDiamond/Martian do learned quality routing but are cloud-API-centric and do not measure your hardware; Portkey/LiteLLM are mature managed/self-hostable gateways with far more provider coverage, observability, guardrails and enterprise hardening than hibrid. Where competitors are genuinely stronger: published, peer-reviewed savings benchmarks (RouteLLM ~85% on MT Bench at 95% GPT-4 quality), provider breadth (OpenRouter 400+, Portkey 1600+ models), enterprise features, and funding/maturity (Martian near a reported $1.3B valuation, Accenture-backed).

field_benchmarks.name: RouterBench
field_benchmarks.what: Benchmark for evaluating multi-LLM routing systems (~405k inference outcomes)
field_benchmarks.headline_numbers: Establishes routing can save cost without quality loss; original paper light on exact %

field_benchmarks.name: LLMRouterBench (2026)
field_benchmarks.headline_numbers: Flagship routers (GraphRouter PF, Avengers-Pro) up to ~31.7-32% cost reduction with no accuracy loss; notes HybridLLM/FrugalGPT/OpenRouter sometimes fail to beat BestSingle

field_benchmarks.name: FrugalGPT
field_benchmarks.what: Cascade approach: query weak->strong until a quality judge accepts (Chen, Zaharia, Zou 2023)
field_benchmarks.headline_numbers: Widely cited: up to ~98% cost reduction while matching best single LLM (GPT-4) on some datasets (mark: classic paper claim, dataset-dependent)

field_benchmarks.name: RouteLLM (MT Bench)
field_benchmarks.headline_numbers: ~85% cost cut on MT Bench at 95% GPT-4 quality; ~45% MMLU; ~35% GSM8K

field_benchmarks.name: GPTCache / semantic caching
field_benchmarks.what: Reuse of semantically similar prior answers
field_benchmarks.headline_numbers: ~30-70% inference cost reduction; up to 68.8% fewer API calls in one study

caveats: Martian and Not Diamond market cost/quality gains but no first-party numeric savings benchmark was located in the sources searched; marked uncertain.
FrugalGPT's ~98% figure is the classic paper claim and is highly dataset-dependent; LLMRouterBench (2026) reports FrugalGPT sometimes fails to beat the best single model.
Some 'local-first' partials (LiteLLM, Portkey, semantic-router, GPTCache) can use local models but are not designed as local-first decision engines the way hibrid is.
Ollama v0.14 (Jan 2026) added direct Anthropic Messages API compatibility, lowering the barrier for tools like Claude Code to call local models without a proxy - relevant context for hibrid's niche.
